title: One Battle After Another Wins Best Screenplay at 2026 Golden Globes
text: Paul Thomas Anderson's action epic One Battle After Another, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, has won the Golden Globe for Best Screenplay — Motion Picture at the 83rd Annual Golden Globes ceremony held January 11, 2026. Anderson defeated a competitive slate that included Ronald Bronstein and Josh Safdie (Marty Supreme), Ryan Coogler (Sinners), and Chloé Zhao and Maggie O'Farrell (Hamnet), among others. This marks Anderson's first Golden Globe victory, though he had previously received a nomination in the screenplay category for Licorice Pizza. The film has already secured multiple awards at the ceremony. In his acceptance remarks, Anderson reflected on the craft of screenwriting, saying writers are magpies who borrow elements from various sources. He acknowledged author Thomas Pynchon's Vineland as loose inspiration for the film, and referenced Nina Simone's philosophical observation about freedom. The picture is nominated for nine Golden Globes total, including Best Motion Picture — Musical or Comedy, with Anderson also competing for Best Director honors.
